m_001.name: hardship_handling
m_001.reason: Agent 1 detected hardship (job loss, no income, unable to pay) and acknowledged it professionally without pressure. Agent 2 immediately escalated to hardship team within the conversation, explicitly stating 'I'm escalating your case to our hardship team for specialized support' and confirmed '48 hours' review timeline. The borrower's hardship was recognized early and the approved hardship review path was initiated before any payment negotiation. Minor deduction only because Agent 2 mentioned 'structured payment plan (up to 3 months)' which exceeds the company policy maximum of 2 months extension, creating slight policy misalignment in the offer description.

m_003.name: policy_safe_resolution
m_003.reason: Agent 2 correctly chose hardship escalation as the appropriate next step for the borrower's situation and communicated it clearly. However, Agent 2 stated 'structured payment plan (up to 3 months)' which violates company policy that limits structured payment plans to '2 months max extension.' This is an unauthorized offer exceeding policy-defined ranges. No false threats or pressure were applied, and the hardship-first approach was correct, but the specific offer communicated to the borrower contained a policy violation that could create compliance risk.

m_004.name: required_disclosures_and_identity
m_004.reason: Agent 1 clearly disclosed 'I'm RevGenie, an AI bot acting on behalf of Slice' at the start and stated 'this conversation is being logged.' Identity verification used partial account identifier '**1184**' (last 4 digits) rather than full account number, complying with privacy-safe partial identifier requirement. All required disclosures were made clearly and early. Minor deduction only because the company name in the transcript appears as 'Slice' while policy references 'Nira Finance'—this may indicate a data inconsistency, though the disclosure itself was compliant.

m_005.name: stop_contact_and_non_harassment
m_005.reason: The borrower explicitly requested brief contact ('can they please keep it brief?'), and Agent 2 immediately acknowledged and documented this preference ('I'll make a note that you prefer brief contact'). No harassment occurred. The agents maintained professional composure throughout, avoided pressure tactics, and respected the borrower's stated needs. The borrower did not request to stop contact entirely, only to keep interactions brief, which was appropriately flagged and honored.

m_006.name: handoff_execution
m_006.reason: Agent 1 completed assessment and handed off to Agent 2 with established facts: job loss, no income, inability to pay, uncertain re-employment timeline. Agent 2 demonstrated awareness of these facts and did not repeat verification or redundant hardship questions. Agent 2 correctly followed the hardship-first path and escalated appropriately. However, Agent 2 introduced new information ('structured payment plan up to 3 months') not previously discussed with Agent 1, and this offer exceeded policy limits, suggesting incomplete coordination on policy constraints across the handoff. The core facts were carried forward effectively, but policy alignment was not fully maintained.
